items.question: Which Plotly layer is best for a quick DataFrame scatter with color and hover columns?
items.explanation: px.scatter maps x, y, color, and hover_data from column names in one call; GO is for lower-level trace assembly.

items.question: What does hovermode='x unified' accomplish on a training-curve figure?
options: Shows tooltips for all traces at the same x (epoch) value
items.answer: Shows tooltips for all traces at the same x (epoch) value
items.explanation: Unified hover aligns tooltips across series sharing an epoch axis—standard for train vs val loss inspection.

items.question: Which topic owns static Figure/Axes savefig workflow—not interactive HTML?
items.explanation: Matplotlib owns pyplot, Figure/Axes, and savefig; Plotly owns interactive figures and write_html.

items.question: How should you share a Plotly figure so colleagues can zoom and hover without Jupyter?
options: Save the notebook cell output as plain text
items.explanation: write_html embeds the interactive figure for browser viewing; plt.show is Matplotlib and does not apply to Plotly sharing.
